value_proposition: Provide repository owners with a fail-closed, start-bound implementation lease whose authority and recovery behavior can be independently verified.
target_segment: Maintainers and product owners using TEMPO to supervise coding-agent implementation

target_user.description: A repository owner or reviewer who needs a clear answer to whether an implementation write is currently authorized.
target_user.value_or_need: One consistent status and receipt contract across authorization, start, guard, failure, restart, and revocation.

economic_buyer.description: The team accountable for security incidents, audit ambiguity, and agent rework.
economic_buyer.value_or_need: Lower review and incident-recovery cost from deterministic fail-closed lifecycle behavior.

willingness_to_pay_or_internal_roi_hypot: A team will continue using TEMPO when stronger lease guarantees remove product-specific wrapper checks without increasing normal setup materially.
revenue_or_economic_value_model: Value comes from avoiding unauthorized implementation, manual state repair, duplicated downstream guard logic, and ambiguous code-review evidence.
acquisition_distribution_hypothesis: The fix will reach existing users through a small upstream pull request, release notes, and the unchanged TEMPO CLI rather than a separate coupled package.
adoption_friction: Existing tests and scripts that treat authorization as equivalent to start must be updated
A current human warrant is still required for implementation
Repository identity binding introduces explicit migration data
key_partners_or_dependencies: Existing TEMPO schemas and ledger verifier

rough_cost_drivers.basis: Three focused implementation and verification cycles across lifecycle and guards
Migration of tests and demo expectations from authorized to started build authority

alternatives_and_differentiation.differentiation: The upstream framework remains the single authority source and products consume only receipts.
Failure-atomic lifecycle operations eliminate authoritative split state instead of documenting repair steps.

alternatives_and_differentiation.alternative: Treat a warrant as immediate build permission
alternatives_and_differentiation.differentiation: A unique committed start becomes a distinct lease boundary with task and repository identity.

defensibility_hypothesis: The durable value is a small deterministic kernel with hash-bound evidence, explicit human authority, and machine-verifiable lifecycle receipts rather than policy prose alone.

material_risks.description: A compatibility shortcut could preserve an authorization-only bypass.
A failed lifecycle command could leave state, active, and ledger inconsistent.
Repository binding could break portable fixture and demo workspaces.
